title: Showing how I use AI in live trading automation (equities, options, crypto) — doing a live demo next week Tuesday at 12p ET
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
Been seeing a lot of threads lately about AI + trading automation. Most of it stopping at theory -signals, dashboards, backtests, etc.

So next week I'm doing a live demo showing what it actually looks like end-to-end.

Taking a strategy idea in plain English → generating working code → connecting it to a real trading API → trading it live across equities, options, and crypto.

Open for live Q&A, so if you’ve been experimenting with AI/agents in trading, drop your questions!

Join:  
[https://public.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN\_BMFxX17JTPyCOJ2SuW1r1w](https://public.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_BMFxX17JTPyCOJ2SuW1r1w)

comments.author: TexasGater
comments.body: i work 3rd shift and will not be able to watch live. will it be recorded for later viewing?

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: For sure-going to record and post on YouTube

replies.author: Historical_Flight873
replies.body: How can i find the link of youtube?

comments.author: Front-Audience-4045
comments.body:
This is the demo format that actually matters — end-to-end live execution is where most AI trading content falls apart, so curious to see how you've handled the API layer and latency in real conditions.



I build AI trading agents across equities, options, and crypto professionally, so I'll have some pointed questions ready for the Q&A. Will be there.

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: The webinar was today if you missed it- dm me your questions happy to help

comments.author: Witty-Dependent-7275
comments.body: I've been running algo strategies for about a year now. The biggest lesson is backtesting ≠ live performance — slippage and timing eat into profits way more than expected. What timeframe are you targeting?
